page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table sheds light on the age at first consultation among individuals diagnosed with various conditions related to 5α-reductase type 2 deficiency, including Partial Androgen Insensitivity Syndrome (PAIS) and cases classified as Undetermined. The mean age at first consultation for patients with 5α-reductase type 2 deficiency is reported as 9.6 years, with a standard deviation of 7.5 years, indicating a relatively broad age range from early childhood to late adolescence. In contrast, individuals with PAIS present at an average age of 8.0 years, while those in the Undetermined category consult at an even younger average of 2.5 years. The significant p value of 0.02 suggests a statistically meaningful difference in the age at first consultation across these groups, highlighting the urgency for timely diagnosis and intervention. Furthermore, the inclusion of hazard ratios and corresponding 95% confidence intervals in the analysis provides a deeper understanding of the risks associated with delayed consultation in these populations. This information is critical for healthcare professionals aiming to improve early detection and management strategies for patients affected by these conditions.
